experiences.intentions: There are two boxes: at (2,1) and (3,4). The box at (2,1) is closest to target (1,1) and can be solved immediately by pushing it up, since the player at (1,2) can move to (2,2) and then push left/up later. But right now from (1,2), moving left to stand on target (1,1) would block that route less usefully. Better is to start repositioning toward the left box: move down to (2,2), which sets up a possible push left on the next turn to shift the box away from the wall? Actually box (2,1) can only be pushed up from below or down from above impossible due to wall adjacency; from current layout, if player moves to (2,2), then could push left into wall column? No. The solvable box is likely (3,4) toward target (4,2), but needs repositioning. Immediate safe move is down to open access through center without risking deadlock. This begins navigating around boxes while preserving options.
